wines.name: Saint-Joseph Le Grand Pompee Rouge
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak casks

taste.summary: Jaboulet's flagship Saint-Joseph, widely available and reliable. The Le Grand Pompee introduces the appellation's Syrah character at an accessible entry price.

pairings.why: Accessible Syrah and a quality lamb burger are a straightforward match.
The wine's herbal and moderate fruit character complements pork terrine.
Earthy mushrooms and the wine's herb notes are natural companions.

history.summary: Le Grand Pompee has been the Jaboulet house's entry Saint-Joseph since the 1970s, providing a reliable and affordable introduction to the appellation.

milestones.event: Jaboulet introduces Le Grand Pompee as the house Saint-Joseph expression

wines.description: Jaboulet's entry Saint-Joseph rouge. Reliable, peppery Syrah at an accessible price introducing the appellation's Northern Rhone character.

wines.name: Hermitage Blanc Chevalier de Sterimberg
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French oak casks

taste.summary: Named for the Chevalier de Sterimberg who legend says planted the first Hermitage vines, Jaboulet's white Hermitage is a benchmark Marsanne and Roussanne blend with ageing potential.

pairings.why: The wine's mineral richness matches lobster and cream-based seafood.
Delicate rich sweetbreads and the wine's hazelnut mineral register create harmony.
The wine's almond and peach notes echo the tart filling.

history.summary: Named for the Crusader knight who according to legend planted the first Hermitage vines on the hill in the 13th century, the Chevalier de Sterimberg blanc has been Jaboulet's benchmark white Hermitage for generations.

milestones.event: Jaboulet introduces Chevalier de Sterimberg as the house white Hermitage

wines.description: Jaboulet's white Hermitage named for the legendary Crusader who planted the first vines. Marsanne and Roussanne blend with waxy mineral complexity and excellent ageing potential.

wines.name: Hermitage Cuvee Cathelin
wines.oak_regime: 18-24 months in old French oak foudres

taste.finish: extraordinary, minerals and dried flowers that persist for minutes
taste.summary: The rarest wine in the Northern Rhone. Cuvee Cathelin is made only in exceptional vintages from a single lieu-dit selection on the Hermitage hill. Named for the painter Jean Cathelin who collaborated with the domaine.

pairings.dish: A perfect roast rack of lamb with 30 years of age on the wine
pairings.why: Mature Hermitage and roast lamb is the canonical Rhone pairing.
Luxury game and truffle can approach the complexity of this extraordinary wine.

history.summary: Cuvee Cathelin was first produced in 1990 from a single selection of the finest barrels of Hermitage, named for the painter Jean Cathelin who created the domaine's label artwork. It is made only in extraordinary vintages: 1990, 1991, 1995, 1998, 2000, 2003, 2009, 2010, 2013, 2015.

milestones.event: First vintage of Cuvee Cathelin from the finest barrels of Hermitage
Second Cathelin produced; establishes the rarity and selectivity of the cuvee

wines.description: The rarest Hermitage. Cuvee Cathelin is made only in exceptional vintages from the finest single-parcel selection. Named for painter Jean Cathelin. Among the most collectible Northern Rhone wines ever produced.

wines.name: Ermitage Ex-Voto Blanc
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: The white companion to Guigal's Ex-Voto rouge, from old Marsanne and Roussanne vines on the Hermitage hill. Massively rich and textured with extraordinary ageing potential.

pairings.dish: Whole roast turbot with brown butter
pairings.why: The wine's opulent richness and golden fruit are ideal with whole-roasted finest fish.
Rich foie gras and the wine's golden, rich character create a special occasion pairing.

pairings.dish: Risotto with white truffle and butter
pairings.why: The wine's exotic spice and mineral depth complement the luxury of white truffle.

history.summary: Ex-Voto Blanc was first produced in 2007 alongside the rouge version. From old Marsanne and Roussanne vines on the Hermitage hill, it is the richest and most concentrated white Hermitage produced by the Guigal house.

milestones.event: First vintage of Ermitage Ex-Voto Blanc from old Marsanne and Roussanne vines

wines.description: Guigal's prestige white Hermitage, first made in 2007. Old Marsanne and Roussanne of extraordinary richness and concentration from the granite hill of Tain.

wines.name: Saint-Joseph Rouge
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak casks

taste.summary: Guigal's entry Saint-Joseph, widely available and reliable. A light, fresh introduction to Northern Rhone Syrah with characteristic pepper and violet aromas.

pairings.dish: Grilled chicken kebabs with lemon herbs
pairings.why: Light Syrah suits herbal, grilled poultry preparations.
The wine's fresh fruit and acid balance make it a good casual pizza wine.
Entry Syrah and French charcuterie are the everyday Rhone pairing.

history.summary: Guigal's Saint-Joseph has been a reliable entry-level offering in the house's range for decades, providing broad access to the Northern Rhone Syrah character.

milestones.event: Guigal begins producing Saint-Joseph as part of the house's Northern Rhone range

wines.description: Guigal's entry-level Saint-Joseph rouge. Fresh, peppery Syrah providing broad access to Northern Rhone character at an accessible everyday price.

wines.name: Cote-Rotie Terres Sombres
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new and used French oak barriques

taste.summary: Cuilleron's flagship Cote-Rotie, expressing the dark, smoky mineral character of the Cote Brune parcels. Terre Sombre means dark earth, reflecting the iron-rich schist soils.

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and mineral character match slow-cooked lamb.
Black olive notes in the wine echo the preparation while dark fruit matches duck.

pairings.dish: Venison ragout with herbes de Provence
pairings.why: Game and herbs echo the wine's dark mineral and herbal character.

history.summary: Terres Sombres is Cuilleron's Cote-Rotie from the Cote Brune sector, expressing the dark, iron-mineral character of the darker schist soils that distinguish the Brune from the lighter Blonde slope.

milestones.event: First vintage of Terres Sombres as Cuilleron's flagship Cote-Rotie

wines.description: Cuilleron's flagship Cote-Rotie from Cote Brune parcels. Dark, iron-mineral Syrah expressing the 'sombre' (dark) character of the schist soils.

wines.name: Condrieu Coteau de Chery
wines.oak_regime: 8 months in new and used French oak barrels

taste.summary: Chapoutier's single-vineyard Condrieu from the Coteau de Chery, one of the most prized sites in the appellation. Biodynamically farmed Viognier with tropical aromatics and excellent depth.

pairings.dish: Seared scallops with mango and coriander
pairings.why: The wine's tropical mango character echoes the fruit in the scallop preparation.

pairings.dish: Foie gras terrine with fig chutney
pairings.why: The wine's opulent fruit and honey profile matches foie gras richness.
The wine's ginger and spice notes resonate with Moroccan spicing on chicken.

history.summary: The Coteau de Chery is a specific amphitheatre-shaped site in Condrieu that concentrates Viognier's aromatics through exceptional sun exposure and granite soils. Chapoutier added it to their single-vineyard range in 1997.

milestones.event: First vintage of Coteau de Chery from Chapoutier's biodynamic Condrieu parcels

wines.description: Chapoutier's biodynamic single-vineyard Condrieu from the Coteau de Chery. Lush tropical Viognier with honeysuckle, mango, and ginger aromatics.

verified.address_quoted: 18 Avenue du Docteur Paul Durand, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage

wines.name: Crozes-Hermitage Clos des Grives Blanc
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak barriques

taste.summary: The white Clos des Grives is biodynamically farmed Marsanne and Roussanne producing a rich, textural Crozes white at excellent value. One of the appellation's finest whites.

pairings.why: Rich Marsanne-based white suits cream and herb preparations with poultry.
The wine's mineral freshness and peach fruit balance the richness of crab.
Hazelnut and mineral Marsanne white suits earthy cream pasta.

history.summary: The white Clos des Grives was added to Combier's single-parcel range in 1993, reflecting the biodynamic estate's desire to showcase both the red and white potential of their Crozes terroir.

milestones.event: First vintage of Clos des Grives blanc from biodynamic Marsanne and Roussanne

wines.description: Combier's biodynamic white Crozes from the Clos des Grives parcel. Marsanne and Roussanne blend with hazelnut and mineral character at excellent value.

wines.name: Condrieu Le Grand Vallon Vendanges Tardives
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in new French oak barriques

pairings.why: Sweet Viognier and blue cheese create a rich contrast.
The wine's peach and caramelised apricot notes echo the classic French tart.

pairings.dish: Duck liver pate with apricot compote
pairings.why: The wine's tropical fruit sweetness balances the rich liver pate.

history.summary: The late-harvest Condrieu from Villard was first produced in 1998, a year of exceptional ripeness in the Northern Rhone. It is produced only in warm years when the Viognier achieves exceptional concentration.

milestones.event: First vintage of Vendanges Tardives from overripe Viognier in an exceptional warm year

wines.description: Villard's rare late-harvest Condrieu from exceptional warm years. 100% Viognier with candied tropical fruit, honey, and exceptional aromatic concentration.

wines.name: Crozes-Hermitage Les Lises
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in used French oak barriques

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and mineral character are at home with classic French lamb.
Earthy mushrooms and dark fruit combine beautifully with this mineral Crozes.
The wine's mineral depth and dark fruit match the juniper-game combination.

history.summary: Les Lises is the name of the domaine and the prestige cuvee, from the oldest and most mineral parcels of the estate in Mercurol. Maxime Graillot's most ambitious expression of Crozes-Hermitage.

milestones.event: Les Lises first produced as the prestige cuvee from the domaine's oldest vine parcels

wines.description: Maxime Graillot's prestige single-vineyard Crozes-Hermitage. Concentrated and precise mineral Syrah from the domaine's oldest vines, showing the next generation of Northern Rhone talent.

wines.name: Crozes-Hermitage Blanc
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's fresh mineral character complements delicate white fish.

pairings.dish: Mussels in white wine and cream
pairings.why: Classic shellfish and Northern Rhone white is a reliable pairing.

pairings.dish: Gratin de courgettes with goat cheese
pairings.why: The wine's stone fruit and minerals balance a simple vegetable gratin.

history.summary: Graillot's Crozes blanc has been produced alongside the red since the late 1980s. Like the red, it has established itself as a reliable benchmark for the appellation at excellent value.

milestones.event: Graillot begins producing Crozes blanc alongside the flagship red

wines.description: Graillot's Crozes blanc, widely regarded as one of the best-value whites in the Northern Rhone. Marsanne and Roussanne with stone fruit and mineral character.

wines.name: Chateau de Beaucastel Chateauneuf-du-Pape Rouge
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in large French oak foudres

taste.summary: A structured Mourvedre-led Chateauneuf with deep garnet colour, garrigue and leather on the nose, plush fruit on the palate and a firm, mineral finish.

pairings.dish: Leg of lamb with rosemary and garlic
pairings.why: Mourvedre's iron-and-herb profile is a classic match for roasted lamb.
Gamey, rich meat needs the wine's tannic grip and earthy depth.

pairings.dish: Aged Comtom or Tome de Chevre
pairings.why: Firm tannins and high ABV cut through fatty aged cheese.
The wine's garrigue notes echo the olive and herbal braise.

history.summary: Pierre Tramier planted the estate in 1909; the Perrin family acquired it in 1950. Beaucastel is the only Chateauneuf estate to cultivate all 13 permitted grape varieties. The unusually high Mourvedre content distinguishes the wine from Grenache-dominant peers.

milestones.event: Estate founded by Pierre Tramier; early plantings of all permitted Chateauneuf varieties
Perrin family acquires the estate
Jacques Perrin begins bottling estate wines under chateau name

wines.description: Flagship Mourvedre-led Chateauneuf-du-Pape from the Perrin family estate, one of the appellation's most age-worthy and complex blends.

wines.name: Chateau de Beaucastel Chateauneuf-du-Pape Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Partial new oak barrique, remainder old 600L demi-muids

taste.summary: Roussanne-dominant white Chateauneuf with a lush, waxy texture, evolved aromas of beeswax and orchard fruit, and a mineral saline finish that ages magnificently.

pairings.why: Roussanne's rich texture and saffron-honey character mirrors the saffron-laden broth.
The wine's herbal anise notes echo the fennel; full body handles the fish's richness.
Waxy Roussanne is a classic match for white truffle.
Creamy texture and weight match well with soft-rind cheese.

history.summary: The white Beaucastel is dominated by Roussanne, an unusual choice for the Rhone where Grenache Blanc typically leads. The Perrins ferment in a mix of old barrique and demi-muids, producing a wine that evolves dramatically over 10-20 years.

milestones.event: White Beaucastel begins as an estate wine; Roussanne-led blend
Roussanne Vieilles Vignes bottled separately for the first time

wines.description: Roussanne-led white Chateauneuf-du-Pape from Beaucastel, known for its extraordinary longevity and waxy, complex texture.

wines.name: Hommage a Jacques Perrin
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in large French oak foudres

taste.summary: Mourvedre-dominant prestige cuvee made in exceptional years only; extraordinary iron, meat, and herb complexity with massive structure and decades of ageing potential.

pairings.dish: Roasted leg of lamb with black olive tapenade
pairings.why: The wine's olive and iron notes are a direct echo of the dish.
Wild game demands the depth and iron spine of Mourvedre.
Concentration and structure hold their own against truffle and red meat.

history.summary: Created by the Perrin family in memory of patriarch Jacques Perrin, produced only in exceptional years from the oldest Mourvedre vines on the estate.

milestones.event: First vintage of Hommage a Jacques Perrin, named after patriarch Jacques Perrin
Second release; production established as exceptional-year only

wines.description: Exceptional-vintage prestige cuvee from Beaucastel, the Mourvedre-dominant homage to patriarch Jacques Perrin.

wines.name: Chateau de Beaucastel Roussanne Vieilles Vignes
wines.oak_regime: Partial new oak barriques; partial old foudres

taste.summary: 100% old-vine Roussanne; the richest and most complex white of the Southern Rhone with extraordinary hazelnut, truffle, and beeswax character and extraordinary ageing capacity.

Full body and creamy texture match the richness of lobster bisque.
Waxy Roussanne weight balances richness of foie gras.
